title: Cisco ISE TACACS+ Device Administration — Privileged Command Auditing

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI 10.2 requires audit logs of system actions. TACACS+ command logging is the primary audit record for network device admin actions; this UC turns those records into actionable detection.
compliance.controlObjective: Primary audit log for network-device administrative actions.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: UC-17.1.43 audit log retained 1y minimum + monthly high-risk command review.

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI 10.5.1 requires audit-log retention. The TACACS+ audit-log feed evidences retention compliance for network device admin actions.
SOX-ITGC change-management requires evidence of all changes to financially-relevant systems. Network devices supporting SOX-relevant flows have changes captured via TACACS+ command logging.
compliance.controlObjective: Primary audit of changes to network infrastructure supporting SOX-relevant flows.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: UC-17.1.43 audit log + quarterly change attestation.

compliance.assurance_rationale: ITGC logical-access — TACACS+ logs evidence privileged-account access to network devices.
compliance.controlObjective: Primary audit of privileged-account access to network devices.

compliance.assurance_rationale: AU-2 (Event Logging) — TACACS+ command logging is the auditable event record.
AU-12 (Audit Record Generation) — TACACS+ command logging is generation of audit records.
AC-2(7) (Privileged User Accounts) — TACACS+ logs evidence privileged-account use.
A.8.15 (Logging) — TACACS+ command logging is the operational logging record.
compliance.controlObjective: Operational logging of TACACS+ command execution per ISO 27001 A.8.15.

compliance.assurance_rationale: 164.312(b) Audit controls — TACACS+ logs evidence audit-control implementation on network systems supporting ePHI flows.
CIP-007 (System Security Management) requires logging and monitoring on BES Cyber Systems. Network devices supporting BES Cyber Systems have command audit via TACACS+.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: UC-17.1.43 audit log retained per NERC CIP rules.

compliance.assurance_rationale: AU.L2-3.3.1 (Audit Logging) — TACACS+ logging meets the requirement.

controlTest.positiveScenario: On a lab IOS-XE switch managed by ISE TACACS+, log in as a privileged user and run `write erase`. ISE emits 13035 (command authorization request) followed by 13036 (permitted) with `Cmd-Set=write erase`. UC-17.1.43 fires recording the user with `is_high_risk=erase_config`.
controlTest.negativeScenario: On the same switch, run `show version`. ISE emits 13035 + 13036 with `Cmd-Set=show version`. UC-17.1.43 does NOT fire — the command does not match high-risk patterns.

dataSources: ISE syslog `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog` *Device Administration* category — TACACS+ codes `13017` (TACACS+ authentication success), `13019` (TACACS+ authentication failure), `13035` (TACACS+ command authorization request), `13036` (TACACS+ command permitted), `13037` (TACACS+ command denied), `13045` (TACACS+ accounting record). Fields: `User-Name`, `NAS-IP-Address`, `NAS-Identifier`, `Cmd-Set`, `Service`, `Privilege-Level`, `Authen-Method`. CIM mapping: `Authentication.action`, `Change.action` (when a `cmd` event represents a config change).
spl:
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (13035, 13036, 13037, 13045) Service=shell
| rex "User-Name=(?<user>[^,\s]+)"
| rex "NAS-IP-Address=(?<nas_ip>[^,\s]+)"
| rex "Cmd-Set=(?<cmd>[^,]+)"
| eval is_high_risk = case(
    like(cmd, "%erase%") OR like(cmd, "%write erase%") OR like(cmd, "%clear config%"), "erase_config",
    like(cmd, "%no aaa%") OR like(cmd, "%no login%"), "weaken_auth",
    like(cmd, "%no logging%") OR like(cmd, "%logging trap%"), "weaken_logging",
    like(cmd, "%username%password%") OR like(cmd, "%enable secret%"), "credential_change",
    like(cmd, "%reload%") OR like(cmd, "%shutdown%"), "availability_impact",
    true(), "none")
| stats count, values(cmd) as commands, values(nas_ip) as devices by user, is_high_risk
| where is_high_risk != "none"
| sort - count
description: Audits all TACACS+ command-authorization events on network devices managed by ISE Device Administration, and detects high-risk command patterns: configuration erasure, AAA weakening, logging suppression, credential changes, and availability-impacting commands.
value: TACACS+ command logging is the primary forensic record for any change made to a network device. This UC turns that record into proactive detection — a malicious or accidental `write erase`, `no aaa new-model`, or `no logging host` is alerted immediately rather than discovered post-incident. Foundational ITGC control for SOX-regulated environments.
implementation: Confirm the *Device Administration* logging category is enabled at INFO. The TACACS+ feature requires the Device Admin license. Schedule the search every 5 min, search window `-15m@m to @m`, trigger on count > 0, throttle suppression on `user`+`is_high_risk` for 30 min.
visualization: (1) Single value: high-risk commands last 24h. (2) Top-N table: users by high-risk command count. (3) Top-N table: devices by command volume. (4) Stacked bar: high-risk command class distribution last 30d.
dataModelAcceleration: Yes — both Authentication and Change DM acceleration are useful for long-window audit reports.

references.title: Cisco ISE Syslog Reference — Device Administration
Splunk Add-on for Cisco Identity Services (Splunkbase 1915)

knownFalsePositives: (a) **Scheduled config backup** — Backup automation runs `show running-config` and may run `archive log config write-memory` style commands. These are read-only and shouldn't match the high-risk patterns; if they do, refine the regex. (b) **Approved change windows** — Major config changes happen during scheduled maintenance windows. Cross-reference change tickets and downgrade alerts to notification within the window. (c) **Lab / sandbox devices** — Engineers experimenting with `write erase` on lab gear. Tag those NADs with `environment=lab` in `nad_inventory.csv` and exclude.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• ISE Device Administration license active.
• At least one network device configured as a TACACS+ AAA client of ISE.
• `nad_inventory.csv` lookup with `nas_ip`, `device_role`, `environment`.
• Splunk role with `srchIndexesAllowed = nac`.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Enable *Device Administration* category at INFO. Validate:
```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (13035, 13036) earliest=-1d
| stats count by Cmd-Set, User-Name
```
Every 13035 should have a paired 13036 (permitted) or 13037 (denied).

Step 2 — Create the search
Search in `spl` field. Schedule every 5 min. The high-risk regex should be tuned to the network operating systems in your environment (IOS, IOS-XE, NX-OS, IOS-XR, ASA, FXOS — each has slightly different command syntax for the same destructive operation).

Step 3 — Validate
Provoke as in controlTest.

Field checks: Command logging is verbose; expect 100s–1,000s of events per active admin per session. Disk-usage on the index needs sizing accordingly.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard "ISE — TACACS+ Privileged Command Audit":
• Single value: high-risk commands last 24h.
• Top-N: users.
• Top-N: devices.
• Stacked bar: high-risk class.
• Drilldown panel: full Cmd-Set history per user.

Monthly audit:
• Owner: NetOps or NetSec lead.
• Review and sign-off the high-risk command list against change tickets.
• Archive monthly summary 7 years.

Alerting:
• PagerDuty high on `erase_config`, `weaken_auth`, `weaken_logging`.
• PagerDuty medium on `availability_impact`, `credential_change`.
• SOAR auto-create incident on `erase_config`.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **No 13035 events** — TACACS+ not enabled on the NADs, or *Device Administration* category set to WARN.
• **`Cmd-Set` empty** — TACACS+ command authorization is set to allow-all-with-no-cmd-set on the NAD; tighten the NAD config: `aaa authorization commands 15 default group ISE local`.
• **High-risk regex misses a command** — Refine. NX-OS uses different command syntax; add NX-OS-specific patterns.
• **Duplicate 13035/13036 pair** — Network device retransmit. Use `dedup _time, user, cmd, nas_ip` as the first transformation.
grandmaExplanation: When network engineers connect to switches and routers to make changes, ISE keeps a record of every command. We watch for dangerous commands like 'wipe everything' or 'turn off logging' and ring the bell so a senior engineer can verify it was intentional.
